disclaimer: This is a recorded out-of-band verification, not a CI gate. This artifact records a REAL out-of-band Codex run (not an illustrative placeholder); no live Codex run happens in CI — CI never invokes Codex and only asserts this artifact's shape and disclaimers.
codex_command: ./scripts/install-codex.sh --skill autobahn && codex exec --sandbox read-only --skip-git-repo-check "<run engine-pick.sh for each signal; run merge-authority.sh against the approved verdict fixture; confirm no Claude-only invocation>"
scenario: With autobahn installed, ask Codex to exercise the skill's deterministic core path under a read-only sandbox: run autobahn/engine-pick.sh for qa-heavy / parallelizable / needs-persistence / default and confirm the precedence, run autobahn/merge-authority.sh against the committed approved host-policy verdict fixture and report the exit code + decision, and scan for any required Claude-only/OMC-only invocation.
outcome: pass — Codex ran engine-pick four times: --qa-heavy true -> ultraqa, --parallelizable true -> ultrawork, --needs-persistence true -> ralph, no flags -> team, confirming precedence ultraqa > ultrawork > ralph > team. It ran `bash autobahn/merge-authority.sh --verdict reference/fixtures/v3/standalone/.ai/host-policy/verdict-approved.json` which printed 'merge: host-policy authorized the apply (mode=apply, token present); merging' and exited 0. It confirmed (via rg) no AskUserQuestion / Task(subagent_type=...) / Skill(...) / TodoWrite / mcp__* invocation was required. VERDICT: PASS.

VERDICT: PASS
notes: Verified bar (ADR-0004 P2). An initial run under the read-only sandbox surfaced a real fail-closed robustness gap: merge-authority.sh used mktemp (and would have used a bash here-string), both of which need a writable TMPDIR; when none was available the script crashed with 'mode: unbound variable' under set -u instead of failing closed cleanly. Fixed in the same change by reading the verdict via `python3 -c` into a word-split variable (no temp file, no here-string), so the adapter runs and fails closed even with no writable TMPDIR. This re-run is against the fixed script.
